title: cornus sanguinea f. variegata
intro: Cornus sanguinea f. variegata, also known as the Variegated Bloodtwig Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its striking variegated leaves and red stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to possess medicinal properties that can aid in wound healing and the reduction of swelling. The health benefits of Cornus sanguinea f. variegata include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. The plant is typically harvested in the summer months and can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use.
